questions.question: A sum of money becomes Rs 1000 in 2 years and 4913 in 5 years on yearly basis compound interest. Find the annual rate of compound interest.

In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle and OM = MD. BM = 12cm and MC = 3cm. Find the diameter of the circle (in cms). ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/ss-sec-14.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1167/image018.png) => AO = OD radius = r => And OM = MD = r/2 => By properties of intersecting chords => AM x MD = BM x MC => AM = AO + OM = r +r/2 = 3r/2 =>3r/2 x r /2 = 12 x 3 => r2 = 12 x 4 =>r = 4√3 Diameter 2r = 2 x 4√3 = 8√3 cm

questions.question: The diagonals of a horizontal square plane PQRS intersect at O. The sides of the horizontal square plane are 20√10 cm. A rod of length 40cm OM is vertically placed at the centre O. What is length MP?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1167/image028.png) => PS = 20√10 => PR = 20√10*√2 => PR = 20√20 => PO = PR/2 = 20√20/2 = 10√20 => Since the rod OM is placed vertically placed at O, the triangle at POM is a right angled triangle at O => PM2 = PO2+OM2 => PM2 = (10√20)2 + (40)2 => PM2 =100*20 + 1600 => PM2 =2000 + 1600 => PM2 =3600 PM = 60 cm

questions.question: In a triangle ABC, PQ is drawn parallel to BC which divides AB in the ratio of 4:5. Area of ∆ABC = 162 cm2. BQ is joined. Find the area of ∆BPQ.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1167/image029.png) => Area ∆APQ / area ∆ABC = AP2/AB2 => Area ∆APQ / area ∆ABC = 42/92 = 16 /81 => Area ∆APQ = 16*162/81 => Area ∆APQ = 32 cm2 Drop a perpendicular from Q to AB such that it touches AB at M. Now area of ∆BPQ = 1/2 BP*MQ Area ∆ APQ/ area ∆BPQ = AP/BP = 4/5 Area ∆BPQ = 32*5/4 = 40 cm2.

questions.question: If 4(sec300 + cot600)2/(cos300 + tan600) = asec2600, then what is the value of 'a'?
When (4x2 - 48x + 187) is divided by '9' it leaves remainder '7' and quotient is '4', then what is the value of 'x'?
questions.explanation: According to the question, (4x2 - 48x + 187) = (Divisor * Quotient) + Remainder (4x2 - 48x + 187) = (9 * 4) + 7 (4x2 - 48x + 187) = 43 (4x2 - 48x + 144) = 0 (x2 - 12x + 36) = 0 (x - 6)2 = 0 x = 6

questions.question: How many numbers are there from 101 to 400 (including both) which are not divisible by 8?
questions.explanation: Numbers which are not divisible by 8 = Total numbers - numbers which are divisible by 8 Numbers divisible by 8: 104, 112, 120, 128, ............. , 400 We know that an = a + (n - 1) x d [Where, a = first term and d = common difference] 400 = 104 + (n - 1) x 8 => 400 - 104 = 8 x (n - 1) => 296 = 8 x (n - 1) => 37 = n - 1 => n = 38 Required number of numbers = 300 - 38 = 262

questions.question: The total numbers of females who have auditioned from city Q are approximately what percent more than the total number of females who have auditioned from city T?
questions.explanation: We have Total number of people auditioned from city R = 5400 Let x be the total number of people auditioned from all the cities. ∴ 18% of x = 5400 ∴ x = 30000 Number of females who have auditioned from city Q = 52% of 21% of 30000 = 3276 Number of females who have auditioned from city T = 38% of 17% of 30000 = 1938 Required percentage = (3276-1938)/1938 x 100 = 69.04% » 69% Hence, option a is correct.

questions.question: 25% of the males and 25% of the females of city P were selected from the auditioned males and females. The males being selected for the show are more than the number of females being selected by
questions.explanation: Number of males who have auditioned from city P = 56% of 24% of 30000 = 4032 ∴ Selected number of males = 25% of 4032 = 1008 Number of females who have auditioned from city P = 44% of 24% of 30000 = 3168 ∴ Selected number of females = 25% of 3168 = 792 ∴ Required difference = 1008 - 792 = 216 Hence, option a is correct.

questions.question: What is the total number of males from cities T and R who auditioned for the singing reality show?
questions.explanation: Number of males from city T and city R auditioned for the reality show = 62% of 17% of 30000 + 51% of 18% of 30000 = 3162 + 2754 = 5916 Hence, option c is correct.

questions.question: The number of people auditioned from city R is what percentage of the number of people auditioned from city P?
questions.explanation: The number of people auditioned from city R = 5400 The number of people auditioned from city P = 24/100 x 30000 = 7200 Required % = 75% Hence, option d is correct.

questions.question: Steve travelled the first 2 hours of his journey at 40 mph and the remaining 3 hours of his journey at 80 mph. What is his average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Total distance travelled by Steve = Distance covered in the first 2 hours + distance covered in the next 3 hours. Distance covered in the first 2 hours = speed * time = 40 * 2 = 80 miles Distance covered in the next 3 hours = speed * time = 80 * 3 = 240 miles Therefore, total distance covered = 80 + 240 = 320 miles Total time taken = 2 + 3 = 5 hours. Hence, average speed = 320/5 = 64 miles per hour. Note: If Steve had travelled equal amount of time at 40 mph and 80 mph, then his average speed will be the arithmetic mean (or simple average) of the two speeds.

questions.question: Two candidates fought an election. One of them got 64% of the total votes and won with 992 votes. What was the total number of votes polled?
questions.explanation: Let the total number of votes be 'V' If one of them gets 64%, then the other will get 36% of the total votes Therefore, 0.64V - 0.36V = 992 0.28V = 992 V = 3542 Hence. Option d is correct

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys a TV set priced as Rs.20000 after getting successive discounts of 15% and 20%. He spends 8% of his cost price on packaging and transport. At what price should he sell the TV set to earn a profit of 12%?
questions.explanation: Net price after two successive discounts = 20000 x 0.85 x 0.80 = 13600 Net cost price = 13600 x 1.08 = 14688 Selling price = 14688 x 1.12 = 16450.56

questions.question: Aman, Daman and Raman purchased an article for the same price. Aman sold it after successive discounts of 40% and 30% while Daman sold it after successive discounts of 45% and 20%. The difference between their selling prices is Rs. 12. Raman sold the article after successive discounts of 15% and 20%. At what price did Raman sell the article?
questions.explanation: Let M be the marked price of the article SP after 40% and 30% discount = M x 0.7 x 0.6 = 0.42 M SP after 45% and 20% discount = M x 0.55 x 0.8 = 0.44 M Difference = 0.02 M = Rs. 12 => M = Rs. 600 Selling price for Raman = 600 x 0.85 x 0.8 = Rs 408

questions.question: If the points A (2, 3), B (5, k) and C (6, 7) are collinear, then the value of k is
questions.explanation: Since the points are collinear, therefore ΔABC = 0 ½ (2(k - 7) + 5(7 - 3) + 6(3 - k)) = 0 2k - 14 + 20 - 18 - 6k = 0 -4k + 24 = 0 K = 6

questions.question: If 378 coins consist of 1 rupee, 50 paise and 25 paise coins whose values are in ratio of 13 : 11 : 7, the number of 50 paise coins:
questions.explanation: Re 1 50p 25p Respective values 13 11 7 So, respective no. of coins (13*1) (11*2) (7*4) Total no. of coins in 1 set = 13 + 22 + 28 = 63 coins Total no. of sets of coins = 378/63 = 6 set No. of 50 paise coins = 22 * 6 = 132 coins

questions.question: 2 years ago, four times Udit's age was equal to five times Jatin's age and the average of their age was 27 years. If the age of their friend Navin is also considered, the present average age of the three becomes 24 years. What will be the average age of Jatin and Navin 2 years from now?
questions.explanation: Let the present age of Udit and Jatin be m and n years respectively. According to the question, 4(m-2) = 5(n-2) => 4m - 8 = 5n - 10 => 5n - 4m = 2 .... (1) Also it is given that, (m - 2) + (n - 2) = 54 => m + n = 58 .... (2) Solving (1) and (2) we will get, m = 32 years, n = 26 years Present age of Navin = 24 X 3 - 58 = 72 - 58 = 14 years Average age of Jatin and Navin two years from now = (28+16)/2 = 22 years

questions.question: A cylindrical container of height 15 cm and base diameter 24 cm contains oil. This oil is to be transferred to 5 similar conical cans and 6 similar spherical cans. The radii of the spherical cans is 5 cm. If the diameter of the base of the conical cans is 8 cm, find the height of the conical cans.
questions.explanation: Total volume of oil = πr2h = π x 144 x 15 = 2160 π cm3 Let the height of each conical can be h. Total vol of 6 spherical cans and 5 conical cans = vol of cylindrical can. 6 x (4/3 x π x 125) + 5 x (1/3 x π x 4 x 4 x h) = 2160 π => 1000 + 80/3 h = 2160 => 80/3 h = 1160 => h = 43.5 cm

questions.question: Which one will completely divide 521 + 522 + 523?

A contractor employed 30 workers to complete a piece of work in 38 days. After 25 days, he employed 5 more workers and work is completed 1 day before the original time duration. How many days it would have taken him more if he would have not employed extra workers?
questions.explanation: Work completed by 30 workers in 25 days = 30 x 25 Remaining days = 38 - 25 = 13 days Now work completed 1 day before, therefore 12 days remaining Now remaining work done by 35 worker in 12 days = 35 x 12 If he had not employed extra workers then work done by 30 workers in say 'd' days Using formula, M1D1 = M2D2 => 30 x d = 35 x 12 => D= 14 days => Work will complete 1 day late if he had not employed extra workers

questions.question: If 2 sin A/(1 + cos A + sin A) = k, what is the value of (1 - cos A + sin A)/(1+sin A)?
questions.explanation: (1-cos A + sin A)/1+sin A = (1-cos A + Sin A)/1+sin A x (1+cos A + sin A)/(1+cos A + sin A) = ((1+sin A)2 - cos2 A)/((1 + sin A)(1 + cos A + sin A)) = ((1 + sin A)2 - (1 - sin2 A))/((1 + sin A)(1 + cos A + sin A)) = ((1 + sin A)(1 + sin A - 1 + sin A))/((1 + sin A)(1 + cos A + sin A)) = 2 sin A/(1 + cos A + sin A) = k

questions.question: If (2a+10b)/(a+2b)=3,then find the value of (a+3b)/(3a+2b)
